Let me also confirm one minor consistency point flagged earlier ‚Äî the "only gate to T3" wording appears (it should say gates T2‚ÜíT3; the spine "gates the tier"). I noticed line 1991 says milestones are "only gate to T3" and line 2006 says "gates T2‚ÜíT3" ‚Äî consistent. But the SPINE bullet header at line 1990 says "Its milestones are the only gate to T3" while ¬ß3.4's equivalent says "gate to T2/Region." Both fine.

I have thoroughly verified all six axes. Here is my report.

VERDICT: **PASS** (with 3 NITs ‚Äî no BLOCKER or SHOULD findings)

The ¬ß3.6 (T2) reframe faithfully mirrors the ¬ß3.4/¬ß3.4.1 (T1) pattern and ¬ß3.2.1 (T0) spine, and is consistent with the locked 3-reputation canon. All structural counts check out: 8 G-rungs (G0‚ÄìG7), 7 transition rows (G0‚ÜíG1 ‚Ä¶ G6‚ÜíG7), 6 Origin rungs (O0‚ÄìO5). All cross-refs resolve. Pacing/trade/no-sim invariants untouched.

EVIDENCE PER AXIS

**1. Estate-domain spine front-and-centre at region scale ‚Äî PASS.** Title (L1972) "T2 ‚Äî The estate's domain expands to the region." The "What this tier IS" block (L1974‚Äì1986) leads with "the HOUSE rising‚Ä¶ domain expands again‚Ä¶ acting FOR THE ESTATE across all three scales at once: the estate, the village, and the region"; estate-rep arc "TRUSTED ‚Üí HONORARY MEMBER of the house" (L1976, L1994, L2048). G-rungs read as house authority (L2012‚Äì2014: "valley-envoy ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí leading house of the region"). Closing throughline (L2064‚Äì2065): "the estate's domain expanding to the region, not as climbing region society." Only the estate spine gates T3 (L1990‚Äì1991, L2006‚Äì2008).

**2. THREE tracks explicit & correctly ranked ‚Äî PASS.** L1988‚Äì2008: "(SPINE) Estate domain & standing ‚Äî the MAIN QUEST‚Ä¶ only gate to T3"; "(SIDE-TRACK A) Village reputation web ‚Äî still OPTIONAL‚Ä¶ ~10‚Äì15%‚Ä¶ NEVER gates the spine"; "(SIDE-TRACK B) Origin reputation ‚Äî NEW‚Ä¶ optional, fully completable, an accelerant‚Ä¶ NEVER gates the spine." Reinforced by the boxed note L2006‚Äì2008. Aligns with ¬ß1.5.4 (L322‚Äì330).

**3. Origin upgraded to one-tier rep side-track with its OWN rungs ‚Äî PASS.** ¬ß3.6.2 (L2070‚Äì2100): prefix `O` "distinct from the estate rung scheme `R/V/G/C/E` and the estate physical stages `E0‚ÄìE5`" (L2075‚Äì2076) ‚Äî no collision (O0‚ÄìO5 vs E0‚ÄìE5 differ by letter). Opens at G2 doubly-earned (L2081, O0 row L2089). Narrative-only / ZERO mechanical gift (L2078‚Äì2079). Carries reunions + G6 Tama payoff (Tama=Otsuru) at O5 capstone (L2085, L2094). Explicitly LIGHT ‚Äî "6 rungs, not 8 ‚Äî it must never read as a second spine" (L2080, L2096‚Äì2100).

**4. ¬ß3.6.1 earned-transition spine mirrors ¬ß3.2.1/¬ß3.4.1 ‚Äî PASS.** Audit rule "no rung is granted for free (mirrors ¬ß3.2.1 / ¬ß3.4.1)" (L2040). Every G0‚ÜíG7 jump (L2052‚Äì2058) has trigger + in-fiction reason + named granter; granters "stay HOUSE-side and escalate (Genemon ‚Üí Lord Munenori / Naoyuki)" (L2042). Gate to T3 = estate-domain milestone "win the region ‚Äî rivals surpassed" (L2058, L2065‚Äì2066).

**5. Tomita & Akagi fleshed out ‚Äî PASS.** Both real and escalating across all rungs via `[RIVALS]` tags (L2018‚Äì2025) and ¬ß3.6.1; Tomita = money/capital, Akagi = honour/precedence (G3 L2021, G6 L2024). Surpassed by G7 ‚Äî "Akagi allied‚Ä¶ Tomita out-competed into commercial d√©tente ‚Äî never killed, canon ¬ßB" (L2025, L2058). Grounded + multi-route, never open war. Aligns with ¬ß1.8 cast (L543‚Äì544) and ¬ß5 T2.3 (L3470‚Äì3503).

**6. Consistency & canon ‚Äî PASS.** ¬ß1.5.1 T2 ladder (L245‚Äì254) and ¬ß5 T2.1‚ÄìT2.3 (L3401‚Äì3503) align on trusted‚ÜíHONORARY MEMBER, the 8-rung shape, the rivals, and Origin O0‚ÜíO5. T2‚âà16h pacing untouched (¬ß4.8.3 L2967‚Äì2990: 960 min = 16.0h; matches L2998 budget table). Trade kept minimal/capped (G0 "trade backbone opens minimally," L2018; Office driven "to the fore," L1993). No management sim (G5 "hard-capped 2‚Äì3-man detail‚Ä¶ ¬ß2.17," L2023). Macrons consistent (kaid≈ç, sekisho, seki). G2 Origin gate + G6 payoff intact. ¬ß3.2/¬ß3.4 read unchanged (no regression).

FINDINGS

- **{NIT, ¬ß3.6.1 G6‚ÜíG7 granter cell (L2058) + framing (L2042)}** ‚Äî The transition-table *granter column* for the capstone reads "**The castle-town authorities**," which on a fast skim conflicts with the cell-above framing "the GRANTERS stay HOUSE-side‚Ä¶ never conferring rank." The parenthetical reconciles it ("the house still **confers your rank**; the castle-town confers regional leadership"), and this matches the locked model exactly (castle-town confers leadership + invites the house in). T0/T1 mirrors keep the granter column purely house-side ("Lord Munenori"). *Fix (optional):* set the granter column to "**Lord Munenori** (house-side rank) *+ the castle-town authorities (confer regional leadership / invite the house in)*" so the column itself stays house-side and the outside party is the qualifier ‚Äî exactly paralleling the V6‚ÜíV7 cell shape.

- **{NIT, ¬ß3.6 SPINE bullet L1990‚Äì1991 vs ¬ß3.4 wording}** ‚Äî ¬ß3.6 says milestones are "the **only gate to T3**," whereas the parallel ¬ß3.4 SPINE bullet says "the **gate to T2 / Region**." Both correct, but ¬ß3.4 names the *destination tier framed as a place*; ¬ß3.6 names the bare tier id. *Fix (optional):* harmonise to "gate to T3 / the castle-town" for exact mirror parity.

- **{NIT, ¬ß3.6.2 O2 row (L2091)}** ‚Äî "friend **Kanta** (met at G0)" ‚Äî Kanta is first met on the *spine* at G0 (off-books consignment), then "rekindled" on the Origin track at O2. The two meetings are intentional (spine porter contact vs. origin-friend reunion), and ¬ß5 T2.4 (L3511) treats Kanta as both an origin recruit and the first porter contact ‚Äî consistent. *No fix needed*; flagging only because a reader could momentarily read it as a continuity double-introduction. The "(met at G0)" parenthetical already pre-empts this.

No BLOCKER or SHOULD issues. The reframe is canon-faithful and ships-ready as-is; all three NITs are cosmetic/parity polish, none change meaning.
